narrative_summary: The BRCA2 missense variant p.Phe2406Leu (F2406L) alters a highly conserved phenylalanine residue in the DNA repair gene BRCA2, a gene in which pathogenic variants predispose to hereditary breast and ovarian cancer. Functional assessment using a mouse embryonic stem cell–based assay reported that the F2406L substitution behaved similarly to wild‑type protein, yet the authors classified the result as uncertain, reflecting a lack of definitive functional impact [PMC7722754]. No clinical case data, segregation analyses, population frequency information, or computational prediction scores were provided in the extracted evidence for this variant. Consequently, the current evidence base classifies BRCA2 F2406L as a variant of uncertain significance, with functional assays suggesting retained activity but insufficient to resolve its pathogenic potential.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article provides functional assay data for the BRCA2 F2406L variant, indicating it behaves functionally in mouse ESCs but its clinical significance remains classified as uncertain (VUS).',
article_mentions.title: A computational model for classification of BRCA2 variants using mouse embryonic stem cell-based functional assays
